title: SystemVerilog interface signals being omitted during synthesis
body:
### Version

0.57+244

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

When using sby for testing an AXI lite module I'm seeing a few different error messages from yosys. With the full file with nothing commented out I'm getting the error message:
SBY 14:58:44 [sby_output/sby_prove] base: formal_axil_slave.sv:192: ERROR: Failed to resolve identifier \AXIL.ARREADY for width detection!

When commenting that offending line out to bypass that error message I get what appears to be the true problem:
formal_axil_slave.sv:125: Warning: Identifier `\AXIL.AWVALID' is implicitly declared.
formal_axil_slave.sv:125: Warning: Identifier `\AXIL.AWREADY' is implicitly declared.
formal_axil_slave.sv:126: Warning: Identifier `\AXIL.WVALID' is implicitly declared.
formal_axil_slave.sv:126: Warning: Identifier `\AXIL.WREADY' is implicitly declared.
formal_axil_slave.sv:127: Warning: Identifier `\AXIL.ARVALID' is implicitly declared.
formal_axil_slave.sv:127: Warning: Identifier `\AXIL.RREADY' is implicitly declared.
formal_axil_slave.sv:129: Warning: Identifier `\AXIL.BVALID' is implicitly declared.
formal_axil_slave.sv:129: Warning: Identifier `\AXIL.BREADY' is implicitly declared.
formal_axil_slave.sv:130: Warning: Identifier `\AXIL.RVALID' is implicitly declared.
formal_axil_slave.sv:170: Warning: Identifier `\AXIL.BRESP' is implicitly declared.
formal_axil_slave.sv:171: Warning: Identifier `\AXIL.RRESP' is implicitly declared.
formal_axil_slave.sv:184: Warning: Identifier `\AXIL.AWADDR' is implicitly declared.
formal_axil_slave.sv:189: Warning: Identifier `\AXIL.WDATA' is implicitly declared.
formal_axil_slave.sv:254: Warning: Identifier `\AXIL.ARREADY' is implicitly declared.

This all compiles with Verilator and is able to pass tests in cocotb, here is the core file set for just the sby test:
[sby_files.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24293800/sby_files.zip)

I run the above with:
sby axil_regs_half_duplex.sby -f --prefix sby_output/sby

This may relate to the following issue due to the SV interface being used at the top level but I'm not sure how it gets parsed in this case:
https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/issues/5366


### Expected Behavior

Signals in the SystemVerilog interface are used for the connections in the design rather than being omitted.

### Actual Behavior

The interface signals seem to be optimised out/removed before they can be used as connections in the design.
